About

Current FocusIndependent research in workflow automation, AI integration, and building internal tools that save hours.

BackgroundCS graduate (Coventry), followed by two years of full-time Mandarin immersion at NTNU, Taiwan.

ApproachPrefer fewer, sharper tools over sprawling stacks. Build it small, test it real, ship it quiet.

I'm a Computer Science graduate with an unusual resume — technical work in chatbot infrastructure, then two years of Mandarin certification in Taipei, and now independent automation development.

Most of what I build lives in the quiet layer between tools — n8n workflows, WhatsApp bots, Notion-to-Discord bridges — the plumbing that makes a team feel faster without anyone noticing exactly why.

I work best in cross-cultural environments where translation matters: between languages, between product and customer, between a messy human request and a clean JSON payload.

Selected Work

Automation, built to stay out of the way.

01
Shipped Product · Multi-modal AI Archived

Monita — AI Financial Assistant

instagram.com/monita.finance
n8nWhatsApp APIGPT-4VisionAudioGoogle SheetsGoogle DriveGmail

Monita was a WhatsApp-native personal finance assistant shipped publicly with a presence on Instagram. Users logged income and expenses across three input channels — typed messages, voice notes, and receipt photos — and received structured reports back in natural language.

Six n8n workflows orchestrated the system end to end: GPT for voice transcription, GPT-Vision for receipt OCR, and a parsing layer that turned "spent 45k on lunch" into a categorized Sheets row. Around it sat subscription registration, validity checks, and weekday/weekend Gmail reminders — held together by a persona layer that kept Monita's voice consistent across every channel.

Why it's archived — access to the official WhatsApp Business API was out of reach, so we built on a third-party gateway. When message delivery became unreliable enough to affect users, we pulled the product rather than keep running on infrastructure we couldn't guarantee. The architecture, the screenshots, and the lesson about platform dependencies all stayed.

In use — WhatsApp
Under the hood — n8n
02
Client Work · Production Pipeline Live

Trixie — Production Pipeline Bot

n8nDiscordNotionGoogle SlidesFrame.ioSlash CommandsWebhooks

Trixie is a live Discord bot built for Neotrix, an Indonesian animation production studio. She bridges Notion (production database), Discord (team chat), and Frame.io (review) so a shot can move from assignment to review without anyone copy-pasting between apps.

A slash command spins up a new project with its own Discord category, channels, and Notion database. Google Slides storyboards are extracted into a selection database for filtering; approved scenes then mirror into Discord forum threads for team discussion. When a shot's status changes in Notion, the thread updates, a Frame.io share link is attached, and assigned members get tagged.

The quiet part — a daily reminder loop nudges anyone with a task still "In progress" once per day. Assignment is half the job; follow-through is the other half, and that's usually the one that gets dropped. Trixie just doesn't drop it.

In use — Discord
Under the hood — Notion & n8n
Inside a single workflow
03
Vendor Tooling · Commerce

Invoice Automation

n8nGoogle SheetsWhatsApp APIEvent-driven

An end-to-end automation that lets vendors generate invoices automatically the moment a customer completes a purchase.

Integrates Google Sheets as the data source with the WhatsApp API as the trigger and delivery channel — the purchase event kicks off the workflow, the invoice arrives in the customer's thread, the vendor gets their weekend back.
